card_analysis.core_mechanic: Each opponent loses X life and you gain X life, where X is the number of Auras you control
triggered_abilities: At the beginning of your end step, each opponent loses X life and you gain X life, where X is the number of Auras you control

emerging_strategies: Curse tribal as Aura subtype synergy

strategic_profile.game_plan_summary: Accumulate Auras to scale Eriette's drain trigger. The engine is Aura count — every card evaluated by whether it increases your Aura count on the battlefield. The deck inverts aura cost evaluation: cheap, low-impact auras that would be bad in generic voltron are excellent because they maximize Aura count per mana spent.

win_conditions.description: Drain opponents for lethal via end-step trigger with high Aura count

build_paths.name: Aura Drain
build_paths.description: Maximize cheap auras to scale the drain trigger

medium: Creature auras that also serve as removal

anti_synergies.description: Non-aura enchantments don't increase Aura count
anti_synergies.reasoning: These are generically powerful but don't feed Eriette's Aura count engine

strategic_constraints.mana_base_requirements: Two-color, low curve, needs early white

power_indicators.notes: Consistent drain clock but vulnerable to enchantment removal

value_inversions.normal_heuristic: Auras are evaluated by the quality of the effect they grant — bigger buffs or stronger keywords are better
value_inversions.inverted_value: Eriette counts Auras, not their effects. A 1-mana aura that grants +0/+1 and a 4-mana aura that grants +4/+4 both count as one Aura for the drain trigger
value_inversions.evaluation_guidance: Rate auras by mana-cost-to-Aura-count ratio. A 1-mana aura is worth roughly the same as a 4-mana aura for Eriette's engine. Always prefer cheaper.

engine_dependencies.engine: Auras you control on the battlefield
engine_dependencies.false_synergy_warning: Lifegain cards (e.g., Ajani's Pridemate) that don't increase Aura count are false synergies — they replicate the drain output without feeding the engine

mispriced_card_examples.why_undervalued: Bad removal spell becomes a 1-mana aura that feeds the drain engine AND removes small threats
Niche protection aura becomes a 1-mana engine piece that also protects Eriette from removal
Mediocre reanimation becomes a cheap aura that feeds drain AND provides death-trigger value
